free_text: Re-measurement of Q0 and k0 values for 14 nuclides
All irradiations were carried out in
the inner and outer irradiation sites of the
The shape parameter, alpha, of the epithermal
neutron spectrum of the inner irradiation site was
measured by the Cd-ratio for multi-monitor method
using six reactions with well-known Q0 values
Aliquots of the 13 elements studied and the Au
comparator, with volume 1mL and approximate mass
100 microg, were prepared with an estimated accuracy
of 1%. Specpure Plasma Standards, 1000 ppm solutions
certified to be accurate to 0.3%, were purchased from
Alfa Aesar, USA for Ag, As, Au, Cd, Cs, In, Pd, Re,
For all the 100 microg standards on filter paper, with
approximate volume 1 mL, the effects of both thermal
and epithermal neutron self-shielding were negligible.
For thermal neutron self-shielding, the worst case
was Cd, for which it was estimated to be 0.1%
the worst cases were As, for which it was estimated
to be 0.08%, and I, for which it was estimated to
